qualifying_org_definition: Any organization including religious organizations; volunteer work must be performed through an organization (not informal one-on-one labor). Court-ordered community service also qualifies. No 501(c)(3) restriction stated; Iowa policy tracks 7 CFR 273.24 federal standard.
verification_instrument: No named state form; caseworker uses 'any reasonable means' per Iowa Employees' Manual Title 7, Chapter I. Client form 470-5674 notifies recipients of the work rule but is not a verification instrument.
hours_formula_detail: Iowa Employees' Manual Title 7, Chapter I explicitly states 80 hours/month minimum; policy specifies 'Do not use minimum wage criteria to determine the number of hours worked,' ruling out the workfare allotment formula.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Iowa policy (Title 7, Chapter I) requires only that volunteer work be 'performed through an organization (including religious organizations)' — no geographic restriction on where the organization is incorporated or based. No Iowa source addresses out-of-state nonprofits specifically. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying organization suffices absent a state restriction, so a CA-based 501(c)(3) should be accepted operationally.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Iowa does not hold any active ABAWD time limit waivers for FY2026. The OBBBA (signed July 4, 2025) tightened waiver criteria to require >10% unemployment, and Iowa's statewide unemployment has remained well below that threshold.
submission_deadline: Report changes (hours dropping below 80) by the 10th of the following month; per policy, once hours are verified the caseworker may not re-verify each month unless a change is reported.
notes:
Iowa Employees' Manual Title 7, Chapter I (revised February 13, 2026) defines qualifying 'work' for ABAWD purposes to include 'unpaid or volunteer work' performed 'through an organization (including religious organizations)' with no MOU, EDP, county assignment, or provider registration requirement. Verification is by 'any reasonable means,' giving caseworkers full discretion — no named state form exists for volunteer hours. Iowa's SNAP E&T program is described as voluntary, so non-participation in E&T does not block the direct volunteer lane. Tier 1 clean.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial search attempted to refute the Tier 1 classification across four angles. (1) Work registration intake: SNAP E&T is confirmed voluntary in Iowa (hhs.iowa.gov E&T page, Iowa Workforce Development); no evidence of a mandatory work-registration intake step blocking the volunteer lane. (2) EDP/case manager requirement: No evidence found of a required Employment Development Plan or case manager assignment before volunteer hours count; the E&T email contact (etverification@hhs.iowa.gov) and hotline (515-281-3131) relate to E&T program participation, not ABAWD volunteer-lane verification. (3) Approved volunteer site list: Iowa's Volunteer Iowa 'Get Connected' platform is referenced only for IHAWP (Medicaid community engagement), not SNAP; no Iowa-specific SNAP approved-site list was found in any source. Multiple national and Iowa sources confirm SNAP volunteer work requires only that the organization tracks the hours — no state-maintained approved list. (4) One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025): The OBBBA expanded the ABAWD age band to 18–64 (was 18–54), removed exemptions for veterans, homeless individuals, and former foster youth, and Iowa enforces statewide with no waivers active. However, the OBBBA did not add any new EDP, site-registration, or enrollment prestep to the volunteer lane — qualifying activities (including volunteer work with nonprofit/public organizations) are unchanged. Iowa's Chapter I was revised February 13, 2026 (the original record's cited version); a newer-URL version at media/13477 exists (General Letter No. 7-I-48) but PDF extraction failed — however, no public-facing source or advocacy group has flagged any new Iowa-specific prestep requirement. Confidence remains high: multiple public sources, the state policy manual, the Iowa Hunger Coalition, Ballotpedia, and multiple benefit-screener sites consistently describe volunteer work as a self-arranged, caseworker-verified path with no mandatory enrollment or approval step for SNAP ABAWD compliance.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): (1) Hours formula: Iowa Chapter I explicitly states 80 hours/month and instructs caseworkers not to use minimum wage criteria — confirmed federal_80. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: Iowa policy is silent on org geography; requires only that work be 'through an organization.' No in-state registration requirement found. Operationally accepts out-of-state 501(c)(3) under federal default. (3) Geographic waivers: Iowa has no active ABAWD county or statewide waivers in FY2026; OBBBA tightened waiver criteria and Iowa unemployment is well below 10% threshold. (4) Submission UX: No online portal for hours reporting found; recipients call county HHS office (1-877-347-5678) or contact caseworker; form 470-5674 instructs reporting hours changes by the 10th of the following month; once verified, caseworker need not re-verify monthly unless change reported — submission is paper/phone via county office. (5) Form fields: No named state verification form exists; caseworker documents by 'any reasonable means'; default baseline fields apply — no EIN, notarization, DOB, or case number are mandated by any Iowa-specific instrument.
